mechanism_narrative: Sustained declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor demand, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the predictive Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: The joint contraction of housing starts and retail sales indicates a synchronized demand shock that reduces corporate revenue expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon when both series decline simultaneously.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both decline month-over-month
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive or neutral when both housing starts and retail sales contract, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in a multivariate regression over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of high industrial production growth, supply-side capacity expansions sustain corporate profit margins, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as earnings catch up to output.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with high capacity utilization
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns turn negative when industrial production growth exceeds the 75th percentile of historical norms, or the signal loses predictive power during supply-chain disruption episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP accelerates corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as equity markets price in stronger aggregate demand.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns show no positive correlation with GDP acceleration, or the signal generates false buy signals during stagflationary periods where inflation outpaces real growth.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 4.5 percent, borrowing costs compress equity valuations and reduce leverage capacity, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally despite funds rates exceeding 4.5 percent due to strong earnings growth, or the threshold signal produces a negative Rank IC over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous rise in unemployment and decline in nonfarm payrolls confirms a labor market inflection point that precedes broader economic slowdown, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises and PAYEMS falls month-over-month
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive when unemployment rises and payrolls fall, or the joint signal fails to outperform a single-series baseline in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: In a disinflationary environment where CPI growth decelerates while real GDP expands, corporate margin compression eases, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns turn negative when CPI decelerates alongside GDP growth, or the interaction effect reverses sign during supply-side inflation shocks.
